intro: Agarista chlorantha, also known as the Brazilian Agarista, is a flowering plant species native to South America, belonging to the family Actinidiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory infections, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
